name: Early Blight
symptoms: Concentric dark brown spots on lower/older leaves (target-spot pattern)
Yellowing and wilting of affected leaves
Progressive defoliation from bottom upward
Dark lesions on stems near soil line
Leathery dark patches on fruit near calyx
spread_mechanism: Wind-borne spores, rain splash, contaminated seeds and tools
favorable_conditions: Warm humid weather, dense canopy, nitrogen deficiency stress

name: Late Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked irregular dark green/brown patches on leaves
White cottony fungal growth on leaf undersides in humid conditions
Rapid brown-black necrosis spreading across entire plant
Brown firm rot on fruits starting from shoulders
Distinctive foul smell during advanced infection
causes: Fog, mist, or prolonged leaf wetness
spread_mechanism: Wind-dispersed sporangia, rain splash, infected tubers/seedlings
favorable_conditions: Cool nights with fog, continuous rain for 2+ days, temperatures below 25°C

name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White-grey powdery coating on upper leaf surfaces
Distorted, curled, or stunted new growth
Premature leaf drop and senescence
Reduced fruit set and berry quality in grapes
White patches on stems and flower buds
spread_mechanism: Wind-borne conidia (spores), does NOT need free water on leaf surface
favorable_conditions: Warm days, cool nights, crowded plantings, shaded conditions

name: Bacterial Leaf Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked, yellowish stripes along leaf veins
Lesions enlarge and turn white/grey with wavy margins
Entire leaves may wilt and dry out (kresek phase in seedlings)
Milky bacterial ooze droplets on leaf surface in morning dew
Significant yellowing of leaf tips
causes: Wounds from wind damage or insects
spread_mechanism: Contaminated irrigation water, wind-driven rain, infected seed
favorable_conditions: High nitrogen fertilization, flooding, warm temperatures (25–34°C), typhoon damage

name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions with grey centers and brown borders on leaves
Node blast: black discoloration at stem nodes causing breakage
Neck blast: brown-black lesion at panicle base causing grain shriveling
White panicles with empty grains (whiteheads)
Seedling death in nursery beds
spread_mechanism: Wind-borne conidia, infected seed, crop residue
favorable_conditions: Temperature 25-28°C, relative humidity >90%, cloudy weather with drizzle

name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Yellow-green angular patches on upper leaf surface (bounded by veins)
Purple-grey fuzzy fungal growth on corresponding lower leaf surface
Leaves curl downward, turn brown, and drop
Stunted shoot growth and malformed inflorescences
Severe defoliation reducing photosynthesis
spread_mechanism: Wind-blown sporangia, rain splash, oospores survive in soil debris

name: Fusarium Wilt
symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of lower leaves progressing upward (unilateral wilting)
Vascular browning visible when stem is cut longitudinally
Plant wilts during day but partially recovers at night (early stage)
Complete permanent wilt and death
Stunted growth with poor fruit development
spread_mechanism: Contaminated soil, water, equipment; persists in soil for 10+ years as chlamydospores
favorable_conditions: Warm soil (24-28°C), acidic soil pH, sandy loam soils, short crop rotations

name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Bright yellow patches alternating with green on leaves (mosaic pattern)
Complete yellowing of leaves in severe infections
Stunted plant growth and reduced branching
Fewer pods and smaller, shriveled seeds
Delayed maturity by 10-15 days
spread_mechanism: Persistent transmission by whiteflies; not seed-borne or mechanically transmissible
favorable_conditions: Warm dry weather favoring whitefly multiplication, late sowing

name: Anthracnose
symptoms: Sunken dark circular lesions on fruits with concentric rings
Black acervuli (spore masses) visible in lesion centers
Die-back of twigs starting from tips
Leaf spots and blossom blight
Post-harvest fruit rotting during storage and transit
spread_mechanism: Rain splash, infected seeds, contaminated harvest tools
favorable_conditions: Warm humid weather (25-30°C), frequent rainfall during fruiting

name: Stem Borer
symptoms: Dead heart in vegetative stage (central shoot dries and pulls out easily)
White ear/head in reproductive stage (panicle turns white, no grain filling)
Bore holes visible on stems at node level
Frass (sawdust-like excrement) inside tunneled stems
Stem breakage at bore points under wind stress
spread_mechanism: Adult moth flight (attracted to lights), egg masses on leaves
favorable_conditions: Stagnant water in fields, high nitrogen, no natural predators (excessive pesticide use)

name: Leaf Curl Virus
scientific_name: Tomato Leaf Curl New Delhi Virus (ToLCNDV)
symptoms: Upward curling and crinkling of leaves
Yellowing of leaf margins and interveinal areas
Severe stunting of plant height (50-75% reduction)
Flower drop and drastically reduced fruit set
Thick leathery texture of leaves
causes: Begomovirus complex transmitted by whitefly (Bemisia tabaci)
favorable_conditions: Hot dry weather (>30°C), whitefly population explosion, proximity to other host crops

name: Rust (Wheat Rust)
scientific_name: Puccinia striiformis / P. triticina / P. graminis
symptoms: Yellow/orange/brown powdery pustules on leaves and stems
Stripe rust: yellow pustules in stripes along leaf veins
Leaf rust: small brown pustules scattered on upper leaf surface
Stem rust: large dark brown-black pustules on stems (most destructive)
Premature leaf death and shriveled grains
spread_mechanism: Wind dispersal (spores can travel 1000+ km), infected volunteer plants
favorable_conditions: Cool wet weather for stripe/leaf rust (10-20°C), warm humid for stem rust (25-30°C)

name: Black Rot (Crucifer)
symptoms: V-shaped yellow-brown lesions starting from leaf margins
Darkened (black) leaf veins visible when held against light
Wilting and premature drop of lower leaves
Black discoloration of vascular tissue in stems
Soft rot secondary infection in stored heads
spread_mechanism: Seed-borne, rain splash, irrigation water, contaminated tools
favorable_conditions: Temperature 25-30°C, high humidity, insect feeding wounds

name: Citrus Canker
symptoms: Raised corky brown lesions with yellow halo on leaves
Similar crater-like lesions on fruits reducing market value
Premature fruit drop in severe cases
Twig dieback and defoliation
Water-soaked margins around lesions after rain
causes: Leaf miner damage creating entry wounds
spread_mechanism: Wind-driven rain, contaminated budwood, nursery stock, pruning tools
favorable_conditions: Temperature 20-35°C, frequent rainfall, wind, citrus leaf miner activity

name: Sheath Blight
symptoms: Oval/irregular greenish-grey water-soaked lesions on leaf sheaths near waterline
Lesions enlarge and coalesce, forming banded pattern up the plant
White-grey mycelial web visible on sheath surface in humid mornings
Lodging (falling over) of heavily infected tillers
Chalky unfilled grains and reduced grain weight
spread_mechanism: Sclerotia floating on irrigation water, mycelial contact between tillers
favorable_conditions: Warm humid (28-32°C), close spacing, high nitrogen, stagnant water
